action.hypothesis: At the current lower-K anchor (`K=40`) and fair small-capacity setting (`EF=8`), `lowrank_gated_residual` may be undertrained rather than fundamentally weak; dropping the learning rate from `1.6e-3` to `4e-4` while keeping the rest of the recipe fixed is the narrowest exploitation-mode check for whether this family can recover a competitive small-EF proxy point without reopening architecture or EF.
action.summary: Run a proxy stabilization sweep on `lowrank_gated_residual` at `K=40`, `EF=8`, `signum`, and `AUXK_ALPHA=0`, changing only `LR` to `4e-4` to test whether the recent weak fair-comparison result was a step-size mismatch rather than a true quality ceiling.
action.self_review: This follows the runtime exploitation guidance, keeps the single primary variable to LR, respects the EF<=16 / prefer-EF=8 constraint, and continues lower-K work instead of drifting back to EF=32. The main risk is that the family is simply too weak at `K=40, EF=8`, but this is still the least-cost informative check before moving to the next prescribed sweep point.
notes_to_memory: Exploitation-mode follow-up: keep `lowrank_gated_residual` fixed at the fair small-capacity comparison point (`K=40`, `EF=8`) and sweep LR downward before concluding the family is weak there.
This round changes only LR; architecture, K, EF, optimizer, and aux regularization stay fixed to isolate whether the weak `EF=8` result was a step-size issue.
next_hypotheses: If `LR=4e-4` materially improves `K=40, EF=8`, test the remaining untried lower LR (`2e-4`) at the same setting to map the small-EF stability window.
If `LR=4e-4` is still clearly weak, keep the family fixed and move to the next prescribed capacity-efficiency probe at small EF (prefer `EF=12` before any larger EF).
